title: Missed Out On The Lucid Air Dream Edition? You Can Get One On eBay, But It'll Cost You
text: One of the most anticipated electric vehicles started deliveries in October 2021. If you missed out on securing one of the 520 Lucid Air Dream Editions being made, there are now three for sale on eBay Inc (NASDAQ: EBAY).

title: 4 Top Consumer Tech Stocks For February 2022
text: Should investors bet on these consumer stocks for a rebound?

title: Ebay to begin authenticating trading cards sold for $750 or more in the U.S.
text: Ebay Inc. EBAY, +0.20% said Tuesday that it is expanding its "Authenticity Guarantee" to trading cards. The e-commerce site will start with trading cards sold for $750 or more by mid-2022, and will extend the guarantee to those sold for $250 or more after that.

title: eBay Launches Authentication for Trading Cards
text: SAN JOSE, Calif., Jan. 25, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- eBay today announced the launch of its Authenticity Guarantee service for trading cards.

title: eBay To Host Investor Day on March 10, 2022
text: SAN JOSE, Calif., Jan. 24, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- eBay Inc. (Nasdaq: EBAY), a global commerce leader that connects millions of buyers and sellers around the world, will host a virtual investor day on Thursday, March 10, 2022 beginning at 8:00 a.m.

text: I've always liked stocks that have taken a beating.

title: Disney Fans Wait 7 Hours To Buy Figment Popcorn Buckets; Now Selling For $200 On eBay
text: Walt Disney Co (NYSE: DIS) fans, on Friday, waited over seven hours to buy the Figment popcorn bucket on the first day of the EPCOT International Festival of the Arts.  The purple dragon Figment was one of the biggest attractions at the event.

title: In Today's Value-Oriented Market, eBay Will Deliver Strong Gains
text: Shares of eBay have shed 20% of their value since hitting a near-term peak in November above $80. The stock is trading at an attractive ~14x forward P/E ratio.

title: Apple iPods Worth $1M Meant For Native American Students Stolen By A Woman And Put On Sale on Ebay
text: A New Mexico woman has been sentenced to 18 months in federal prison after she stole $1 million worth of Apple Inc (NASDAQ: AAPL)-made music players meant for Native American students. What Happened: Kristy Stock, 62, a former employee of the Central Consolidated School District in New Mexico, admitted that from 2013 to 2018 she stole more than 3,000 iPods, according to a statement from the Department of Justice, first noted on Apple Insider.
